questions.question: What is a Cartesian Product?
questions.explanation: The Cartesian Product is a set of ordered pairs, where each element of the first set is paired with each element of the second set.

questions.question: What is the purpose of a relation between two sets?
options: To find the intersection of the two sets
To find the union of the two sets
To determine if one set is a subset of the other
To define a function between the two sets
questions.explanation: A relation between two sets defines a function, where each element of the first set is mapped to exactly one element of the second set.

questions.question: Which of the following is an example of a relation between two sets A and B?
questions.explanation: A relation between two sets A and B is a statement of the form A ∈ A and B ∈ B, where A and B are elements of the sets A and B, respectively.

questions.question: What is the difference between a relation and a function?
options: A relation can have multiple elements mapped to the same element
A function must have exactly one element mapped to the same element
A relation is a subset of a function
A function is a type of relation
questions.explanation: A relation can have multiple elements mapped to the same element, whereas a function must have exactly one element mapped to the same element.

questions.question: What is the notation for the Cartesian Product of sets A and B?
questions.explanation: The notation for the Cartesian Product of sets A and B is A × B, which represents the set of all ordered pairs (a, b), where a ∈ A and b ∈ B.
